criterion B- WRITE THE NOTES AND TRY ANSWERING ONE SCENARIO
Home task-States of matter
discussions.detail: 8.2 KBAnswer key-1. Why do solids keep their shape while liquids and gases do not? Solids keep their shape because their particles are tightly packed and can only vibrate in one place. Liquids and gases do not have a fixed shape because their particles can move around.2. Which state of matter has particles that move the fastest? Explain why. Gases have particles that move the fastest because they are far apart and have the most energy, allowing them to move freely in all directions.3. Give one example from your da...

discussions.title: Types of Questions
discussions.detail: Home Task – Inquiry Questions-questions related to any science topic (Light, Sound, Forces, Cells, or Matter).Research the three types of inquiry questions:Factual Questions – Questions that can be answered with facts, data, or specific information.Conceptual Questions – Questions that help us understand big ideas, concepts, and connections.Debatable Questions – Questions that may have different viewpoints and do not have one correct answer. assignments.title: criterion B- WRITE THE NOTES AND TRY ANSWERING ONE SCENARIO
Home task-States of matter
assignments.description: 8.2 KBAnswer key-1. Why do solids keep their shape while liquids and gases do not? Solids keep their shape because their particles are tightly packed and can only vibrate in one place. Liquids and gases do not have a fixed shape because their particles can move around.2. Which state of matter has particles that move the fastest? Explain why. Gases have particles that move the fastest because they are far apart and have the most energy, allowing them to move freely in all directions.3. Give one example from your da...
